page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at 3.1 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on a KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a mixture of average and weak performance profiles. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench is categorized as weak, placing in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance under stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests reaches average levels. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register average performance, with read-only small block speeds reaching 402.4 GB/sec. Conversely, database operations, static web serving, and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3 fall into the weak performance tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfc5.2xlarge offers a dedicated compute profile with a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio, making it suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. Tradeoffs include poor performance on larger LLM inference tasks, such as the 7B model, and weak single-threaded execution. This instance is best suited for multi-threaded compute tasks, software compilation, ray tracing, and basic Redis key-value operations, rather than high-throughput web serving or memory-intensive database systems.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring an 8-core Intel Xeon 6149 processor clocked at 3.1 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation with 1 thread per core, 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth, alongside weaker single-core and database execution speeds. The 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not demand high memory capacity. It is best suited for multi-threaded processing, software compilation, and moderate Redis operations, while being less optimal for large-scale database management or single-threaded tasks.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core Intel Xeon compute instance with a one-to-two memory-to-CPU ratio for multi-threaded processing workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
